content: I confirm that I provided comprehensive reports to Mr. Bantjies on **6 June 2025**, exposing the fraud involving Villa Via Arcadia No 2 CC. Mr. Bantjies, as the Applicant's accountant and a concealed trustee, had a duty to investigate these claims. Instead, he dismissed my request for an audit.

The Applicant's claim that discrepancies were "noted" in July is false. I reported them on 6 June 2025. The Applicant's immediate cancellation of 15 business credit cards on **7 June 2025** was a direct act of retaliation and sabotage.

The IT expenses were legitimate and necessary for the R34.9 million e-commerce operation I built. The true irregularities, which I reported, were the R5.4 million stock adjustment, the R900,000 unauthorized transfers, and Villa Via's 86% profit extraction.

My conduct was professional and transparent. I built a profitable global business. The Applicant's characterization of my conduct as "erratic" is a self-serving attempt to justify his retaliatory actions.
The Applicant's claim that payments were "unexplained" is false. I provided comprehensive reports on 6 June 2025.
The card cancellations on 7 June 2025 were a manufactured crisis designed to create the pretext for this application.
The Applicant's cancellation of his own card was punitive theater, designed to create the illusion of a genuine crisis.
The Applicant's claim that transactions remain "unexplained" is false. The reports I provided on 6 June 2025 contained all necessary explanations.
The service interruptions were a direct and foreseeable consequence of the Applicant's own actions.
The Applicant's allegation that I "refused to assist" is false. I had already provided comprehensive assistance on 6 June 2025.
The communications in Annexure PF7 demonstrate my professionalism in the face of the Applicant's manufactured crisis.
The communications with my personal assistant were necessary because the Applicant had created a crisis that required immediate attention.
The Applicant's claim that I "would or could not provide relevant invoices" is contradicted by the comprehensive reports I provided on 6 June 2025.
The interference came from the Applicant and his agents, who systematically sabotaged the business operations.
My email and system access was revoked in retaliation for exposing financial misconduct.
The selective restoration of access was controlled by Ms. Farrar, who was acting under the Applicant's instructions.
The "continued interference" was a systematic campaign of sabotage orchestrated by the Applicant's agents.
The conduct "not in the interests of the corporations" was the Applicant's dismantling of a profitable R34.9 million operation.
The First Respondent's action to obtain duplicate cards was necessary to prevent the total collapse of the business.
The use of personal credit cards to maintain business operations demonstrates the Applicant's bad faith.
The First Respondent defended my professional and transparent conduct.
The Applicant's characterization of the First Respondent's response is a misrepresentation.
I deny receiving a R500,000 "birthday gift." This is a false and unsupported allegation.
The Applicant's reference to the First Respondent's income is irrelevant and designed to undermine her.
Any transfer would have been a legitimate business transaction, not a personal gift.
The Applicant had full visibility of all financial transactions through his agents, Ms. Farrar and Mr. Bantjies.

My remuneration included performance-based compensation for managing the R34.9 million e-commerce operation.

---
The IT expenses were necessary for the R34.9 million global e-commerce operation I built.
The expense categories are standard for a global e-commerce business.
The expense amounts are proportional to the revenue generated.
I discovered and reported the discrepancies to Mr. Bantjies on 6 June 2025.
The investigation was conducted by the conflicted Mr. Bantjies.
The IT vendors are legitimate and well-known providers of e-commerce services.
I provided comprehensive documentation of all IT expenses on 6 June 2025.
International payments are a standard part of a global e-commerce business.
The bank messages regarding foreign exchange limits are standard notifications.

The true financial irregularities are the R5.4 million stock adjustment, the R900,000 unauthorized transfers, and Villa Via's 86% profit extraction.

---
The Applicant's summary is an inversion of the facts.
The bank message is a standard notification, not evidence of misconduct.
The actual misconduct includes the R5.4 million stock adjustment, the R900,000 unauthorized transfers, and Villa Via's 86% profit extraction.

The financial impact of the Applicant's misconduct is substantial.

---
The delinquency application is an abuse of process. My actions were consistent with my fiduciary duties.
The Applicant has grossly abused his position.
The Applicant acted in bad faith and for an improper purpose.
The provisions of the Close Corporations Act apply to the Applicant's own conduct.
The Applicant's conduct meets the definition of delinquency.
The Applicant's conduct meets the definition of probation.
The Applicant and his agents breached their fiduciary duties.
The losses were caused by the Applicant's actions.
The economic benefits were derived by the Applicant and his agents.
The R5.4M stock adjustment suggests systematic fraud.
The mandatory disqualification grounds apply to the Applicant.
Section 162(5)(a) applies to the Applicant.
The intentional harm was inflicted by the Applicant and his co-conspirators.
The Applicant's gross negligence is demonstrated by the card cancellations.
The wilful misconduct includes a six-month campaign of sabotage.
The acquiescence to fraud includes two years of unallocated expenses.
The omissions with fraudulent purpose include material non-disclosures.
The oppressive conduct includes punishing me for exposing fraud.
The conduct inconsistent with duties includes a trustee attacking a beneficiary.
The grounds for probation apply to the Applicant.
The prejudicial results include my exclusion from the business I built.
The oppressive business conduct includes the control of systems by Ms. Farrar.
The oppressive exercise of powers includes the abuse of trustee powers.
The appropriate remedy is a full, independent forensic audit.
The Court's discretion should be exercised against the Applicant.

The unaccounted money includes the R900,000 unauthorized transfers and the R5.4 million stock loss.

---
I built and funded the UK e-commerce operations through my company, RegimA Zone Ltd.
RegimA Zone Ltd is an independent entity, not under RegimA UK Ltd.
I have operational control and funding of RegimA Zone Ltd.
The Applicant had no operational involvement in the UK e-commerce operations.
I built and own RegimA Zone Ltd independently.
UK bank records show that RegimA Zone Ltd continuously paid for the South African Shopify platforms.
The reference to Isaac Chesno is irrelevant.
The Applicant's fear of non-payment is contradicted by bank records.

The Applicant's claim of no financial knowledge is inconsistent with his trustee powers.

---
